Scenario.title: ArcaneDoor Compromises Water/Wastewater Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple Attackers exploited default credentials on internet-facing devices to gain initial access, executed arbitrary commands to maintain persistence, and manipulated network communications to disrupt operational controls.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Command, Social Media Platform
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Command Execution (Mechanism), Bidirectional Communication

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-70: Try Common or Default Usernames and Passwords
stage.description: Attackers leveraged unchanged default administrator credentials on network devices such as routers or SCADA systems, exploiting weak authentication practices to gain unauthorized access to internet-facing assets.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-9: Buffer Overflow in Local Command-Line Utilities
stage.description: After establishing access, adversaries executed arbitrary commands via buffer overflow vulnerabilities in local command-line utilities to escalate privileges and maintain persistent control over compromised systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-325: TCP Congestion Control Flag (ECN) Probe
stage.description: By manipulating bidirectional communication protocols, attackers disrupted network device functionality, isolating critical systems and preventing real-time monitoring or control of water/wastewater operations.

Scenario.summary: ArcaneDoor exploited default credentials on internet-facing infrastructure, escalated privileges through command injection, and disrupted operational systems via network-level manipulation, demonstrating basic but effective targeting of critical infrastructure.
